'''Scott Naylor''' (2 February 1972) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s, and has [[Coach (sports)|coach]]ed in the 2010s and 2020s.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He played at representative level for [[England national rugby league team|England]], and at club level for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford Reds]]/[[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] (two spells) and the [[Bradford Bulls]], as a {{rlp|wg|ce}} or {{rlp|sr}}, and has [[Coach (sports)|coach]]ed at club level for the [[Salford Red Devils]]{{citation needed|date=February 2020}} and in [[RFL League 1|League 1]] and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] for [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Oldham coach Scott Naylor to depart at end of season">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/oldham-coach-scott-naylor-to-depart-at-end-of-season/|title=Oldham coach Scott Naylor to depart at end of season|publisher=loverugbyleague.com|date=7 August 2019|accessdate=1 January 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/scott-naylor-leaves-oldham-with-head-held-high/|title=Scott Naylor leaves Oldham with "head held high"|publisher=loverugbyleague.com|date=7 October 2019|accessdate=1 January 2020}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===World Club Challenge appearances===
Naylor played at {{rlp|ce}} in the Bradford Bulls', champions of [[Super League VI]], 41–26 victory over the [[Newcastle Knights]], premiers of the [[2001 NRL season|2001 NRL]], in the [[2002 World Club Challenge]] at [[Kirklees Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]] in [[Huddersfield]], England, on Friday 1 February 2002, before a crowd of 21,113.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

===Super League Grand final appearances===
Naylor played at {{rlp|ce}} in the [[Bradford Bulls]]' 6-8 defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[1999 Super League Grand Final]] during 1999's [[Super League IV]] at [[Old Trafford]], [[Manchester]] on Saturday 9 October 1999, in front of a crowd of 50,717, he played at {{rlp|ce}} in the 37-6 victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[2001 Super League Grand Final]] during 2001's [[Super League VI]] at [[Old Trafford]], [[Manchester]] on Saturday 13 October 2001, in front of a crowd of 60,164, and he played at {{rlp|ce}} and scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in the 18-19 defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[2002 Super League Grand Final]] during 2002's [[Super League VII]] at [[Old Trafford]], [[Manchester]] on Saturday 19 October 2002, in front of a crowd of 61,138.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

===Challenge Cup final appearances===
Naylor played at {{rlp|ce}} in the [[Bradford Bulls]]' 24-18 victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[2000 Challenge Cup]] Final during 2000's [[Super League V]] at [[Murrayfield Stadium]], [[Edinburgh]], Scotland on Saturday 9 October 1999, in front of a crowd of 67,247, he played at {{rlp|ce}} in the 6-13 defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[2001 Challenge Cup]] Final during 2001's [[Super League VI]] at [[Murrayfield Stadium]], [[Edinburgh]], Scotland on Saturday 13 October 2001, in front of a crowd of 68,250, and he played at {{rlp|ce}} in the 22-20 victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[2003 Challenge Cup]] Final during 2003's [[Super League VIII]] at [[Millennium Stadium]], [[Cardiff]], Wales on Saturday 19 October 2002, in front of a crowd of 71,212.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

===Club career===
Naylor made his début for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the 34-14 victory over the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[1991–92 Regal Trophy]] preliminary round at [[Central Park (Wigan)|Central Park]], [[Wigan]] on Tuesday 29 October 1991, he scored no tries for the Wigan Warriors, and he played his last match for the Wigan Warriors in the 18-0 victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[1991–92 Regal Trophy]] second round at [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]], [[Kingston upon Hull]] on Sunday 6 December 1992.<ref name="Statistics at wigan.rlfans.com">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/readarticle.php?article_id=745|title=Statistics at wigan.rlfans.com|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2019|accessdate=1 January 2020}}</ref>

===International honours===
Naylor won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at the Bradford Bulls in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]] against Australia, Fiji (1-[[Try (rugby)|try]]), Ireland ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]) and New Zealand.

==Honoured at Bradford Bulls==
Naylor was included in Bradford Bulls' 'Bull Masters'.<ref name="Bull Masters - Scott Naylor">{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/content/History/4425/Bull%20Masters%20-%20Scott%20Naylor|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101004182836/http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/content/History/4425/Bull%20Masters%20-%20Scott%20Naylor|url-status=dead|archive-date=4 October 2010|title=Bull Masters - Scott Naylor|publisher=bradfordbulls.co.uk|date=31 December 2010|accessdate=1 January 2011}}</ref>
